content: what is the agentic AI project
what is the agentic AI project

Document 1:
Group 22 CAPSTONE project 
 
I.Project Name:  
Agentic AI Penetration Testing Framework  
 
II.Project Objectives: 
 
The objective of this project is to design and implement an Agentic AI–driven Penetration 
Testing Framework that extends and improve the current proof-of-concept. The student 
team will develop a multi-agent architecture covering reconnaissance, enumeration, 
exploitation, and post-exploitation, while improving reliability by reducing hallucinations 
and redundant scans. The system will provide continuity across sessions, generate 
context-aware exploit strategies, and deliver automated postexploitation analysis with 
structured reporting. The working prototype should demonstrate greater automation, 
accuracy, and usability for AI-assisted penetration testing.  
 
III.Project Overview:  
This is a challenging project that will require students to apply cybersecurity knowledge, 
penetration testing methodologies, and AI/agent-based system design principles. The

**Agentic AI Project – Agentic AI Penetration Testing Framework** The **Agentic AI Penetration Testing Framework** is a capstone project (Group 22) sponsored by George Mason University. Its purpose is to design and implement an **agent‑driven, AI‑powered framework for penetration testing** that goes beyond the existing proof‑of‑concept. Key points from the project documentation: | Aspect | Description | |--------|--------------| | **Goal** | Build a **multi‑agent architecture** that automates the full penetration‑testing lifecycle:
• Reconnaissance
• Enumeration
• Exploitation
• Post‑exploitation | | **Improvements over prior work** | • Reduce AI “hallucinations” and eliminate redundant scans
• Provide **session continuity** so later phases can build on earlier context
• Generate **context‑aware exploit strategies** automatically | | **Outputs** | The system will produce **automated, structured reports** and perform **post‑exploitation analysis** with minimal human intervention. | | **Intended Benefits** | Greater **automation**, **accuracy**, and **usability** for AI‑assisted penetration testing, making the tool more reliable and practical for security teams. | | **Sponsors** | George Mason University (Zhengdao Wang – POC; Md Faisal Ahmed – technical SME)【Document 4】【Document 5】 | | **Project Scope** | The project combines cybersecurity knowledge, penetration‑testing methodology, and AI/agent‑based system design principles【Document 1‑3】. | In short, the Agentic AI project is a research and development effort to create a **self‑directed, AI‑driven suite of agents that can conduct end‑to‑end penetration tests with reduced manual effort and higher reliability**.
